# CoreVPN

> Secure OpenVPN-compatible VPN server with OAuth2/SAML support and ghost mode, written in Rust.

CoreVPN is a memory-safe VPN server that implements the OpenVPN protocol, working with existing OpenVPN clients. It adds modern features missing from OpenVPN: OAuth2/OIDC/SAML authentication (Google, Microsoft, Okta), ghost mode for zero connection logging, TLS 1.3 with ChaCha20-Poly1305, a web admin interface, a native desktop GUI client, and full container/Kubernetes support. Built in Rust for memory safety and performance.

## Links

- [Documentation](https://pegasusheavy.github.io/corevpn/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/pegasusheavy/corevpn)

## Key Features

- OpenVPN protocol compatible -- works with existing OpenVPN clients
- Modern authentication -- OAuth2, OIDC, SAML with Google, Microsoft, Okta support
- Ghost mode -- zero connection logging for privacy-focused deployments
- Modern security -- TLS 1.3, ChaCha20-Poly1305, Ed25519, AES-256-GCM
- Web admin interface -- manage clients, monitor connections, generate configs
- Desktop GUI client -- native GUI built with OpenKit
- Container ready -- Docker, Kubernetes, Helm charts included
- Easy deployment -- DEB, RPM, systemd, OpenRC packages, one-line installer
- Written in Rust -- memory-safe implementation

## Installation

```bash
# Docker (fastest)
docker run -d --name corevpn --cap-add NET_ADMIN --device /dev/net/tun \
  -p 1194:1194/udp -p 8080:8080 ghcr.io/pegasusheavy/corevpn:latest

# From source
git clone https://github.com/pegasusheavy/corevpn.git
cd corevpn
cargo build --release
```

## Quick Start

```bash
# Run with Docker
docker run -d --name corevpn --cap-add NET_ADMIN --device /dev/net/tun \
  -p 1194:1194/udp -p 8080:8080 ghcr.io/pegasusheavy/corevpn:latest

# Access web UI at http://localhost:8080

# Generate a client config
corevpn-server client --user alice@example.com --output alice.ovpn
```
